# Weekly Status Report — Cloud Migration Phase 2

**Reporting Period:** Week of 2026-04-13
**Audience:** Executive Leadership
**Overall Status:** At Risk (IAM blocker + FTE reduction)

## What leadership needs to know
- 3 of 5 workloads migrated; on pace except for IAM integration.
- IAM integration slipped 1 week; decision needed on revised timeline.
- Risk R-042 elevated Low → Medium due to 1 FTE on parental leave.
- Budget healthy at 62% utilization; velocity below plan (34 vs. 40).

## Accomplishments
- Workloads 1–3 migrated (CM-231, CM-232, CM-233 closed).
- Performance testing for workload 3 passed all benchmarks (PT-0414).

## Planned Next Week
- Begin workload 4 migration (CM-241).
- Resolve IAM integration blocker with identity provider team.

## Blockers & Dependencies
- CM-240: IAM integration delayed pending identity provider configuration (owner: IAM team).

## Risks & Issues
- R-042: FTE reduction (parental leave effective 2026-04-20). Likelihood Low → Medium. Mitigation: [NOT PROVIDED].

## Metrics
- Budget utilization: 62% of annual allocation.
- Sprint 14 velocity: 34 points (planned 40).
- Workload migration progress: 3/5 (60%).

## Decisions Needed
- Approve revised IAM integration timeline (+1 week).

## Quality Check
- Missing: mitigation plan for R-042; schedule impact of IAM slip on downstream milestones; test coverage metrics.
- Unverifiable without source: whether velocity drop is solely attributable to FTE reduction.
- Human review required before release: confirm decision authority for IAM timeline change; validate risk rating change with risk owner.
